concerns.text: T013 hardcodes 'EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset' (PhysioNet ID: eegmmidb) as the data source. FR-001 mandates the system MUST identify a dataset containing *paired* resting-state EEG and subjective fatigue ratings. The EegMMIDB dataset contains motor imagery tasks but lacks the required subjective fatigue ratings. This task delivers a solution that cannot satisfy the functional requirement, constituting a silent relaxation of the data validation constraint.

T023 and T024 implement 'topological attractor reconstruction' and 'adaptive simplification vs. degenerative noise' classification. These capabilities are not defined in FR-003 (LZC/PE calculation) or FR-004 (correlation analysis). Introducing these features without a corresponding FR or SC violates the constraint that tasks must not silently relax or expand the spec's defined scope (Constitution Principle I & FR-030).

T027 mandates halting with an error if paired pre/post data is missing. However, plan.md Summary explicitly defines a conditional fallback to 'cross-sectional analysis' if only single-timepoint data exists. This task contradicts the approved architectural approach in the plan, effectively dropping the fallback mechanism required by the design.

SC-001 requires a sample size N ≥ 30 participants. None of the tasks (including T013 for download or T015 for filtering) include a step to validate the final dataset size or re-download/switch sources if the chosen dataset yields N < 30. The task list fails to preserve the success criterion that the pipeline must operate on a valid cohort size.

T019 adds a unit test for 'topological attractor stability metrics'. This test targets functionality (attractor stability) that has no corresponding requirement in the spec (FR-003/FR-004) or user story acceptance scenarios. This is an orphan task derived from scope creep, violating the constraint that tasks must map to defined requirements.
